description: Implementing a custom cipher, MAC, or protocol that passes local tests but fails against real adaptive attackers.
body: Failure mode: an engineer invents or modifies a cryptographic construction; it round-trips correctly in unit tests but leaks under chosen-ciphertext, timing, or padding-oracle attacks that the tests never simulate. Detection signals: a non-standard cipher/MAC/protocol with no public review; comments claiming a clever improvement over a standard; security justified by 'it decrypts our test vectors.' Do not design or tweak cryptographic algorithms. Safer fallback: replace the custom construction with a vetted library's named high-level primitive (AEAD, HMAC, Ed25519, HKDF), keep parameters current, and route any genuine novel need to a cryptography expert review before use.
